---
title: "Projects"
---

A selection of quantitative finance and software projects completed during my studies at the University of Alberta.

<div class="project-grid">

<div class="project-card">
<div class="project-label">QTP &middot; Fin-452</div>
<h5><a href="projects/Alex_Hirtle_1719121.html">WTI Options Portfolio: Full Greek P&amp;L Attribution</a></h5>
<p>Simulation of an energy trading desk risk role. Constructs a complete Greek P&amp;L attribution framework — Delta, Gamma, Vega, Vanna, and Theta — for a portfolio of WTI crude oil options on NYMEX CL futures. Prices options using Black-Scholes and models realistic scenario shifts across underlying price, implied volatility, and time decay.</p>
<div class="tech-tags">
  <span class="tech-tag">R</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Black-Scholes</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Options Pricing</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Risk Attribution</span>
</div>
<div class="project-link"><a href="projects/Alex_Hirtle_1719121.html">View Report &rarr;</a></div>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
<div class="project-label">QTP &middot; Fin-452</div>
<h5><a href="projects/QTP.html">Momentum-Based Sector Rotation Strategy</a></h5>
<p>Dollar-neutral long-short strategy across the 11 SPDR S&amp;P 500 sector ETFs. Applies Kalman filtering to smooth rolling momentum, volatility, and Sharpe ratio features, then uses hierarchical clustering (Ward's D2) to generate monthly rebalance signals. Evaluated over a 24-year training window and out-of-sample period with a full parameter grid search.</p>
<div class="tech-tags">
  <span class="tech-tag">R</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Kalman Filter</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Hierarchical Clustering</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Backtesting</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Momentum</span>
</div>
<div class="project-link"><a href="projects/QTP.html">View Report &rarr;</a></div>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
<div class="project-label">Options Analysis &middot; Fin-452</div>
<h5><a href="projects/p1-options_greeks.html">Options Greeks Analysis</a></h5>
<p>Foundational analysis of options pricing sensitivities through the Greeks framework. Examines Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega, and Rho across varying strike, maturity, and volatility conditions for WTI crude oil options. Includes Greek surface visualizations and explores their practical use in hedging and position risk management.</p>
<div class="tech-tags">
  <span class="tech-tag">R</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Options Greeks</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Black-Scholes</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Hedging</span>
</div>
<div class="project-link"><a href="projects/p1-options_greeks.html">View Report &rarr;</a></div>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
<div class="project-label">Shiny App &middot; Fin-451</div>
<h5><a href="https://github.com/PassiveAlex/Fin-451-Golem" target="_blank">Energy Risk Dashboard</a></h5>
<p>Production-grade Shiny dashboard built with the Golem framework for energy commodity risk managers. Covers WTI (Cushing &amp; Houston), Brent, Heating Oil, RBOB, and Natural Gas futures. Modules include forward curve analysis, rolling minimum-variance hedge ratios with basis risk decomposition, GARCH volatility regimes, EIA petroleum fundamentals, and U.S. Treasury yield curves. EIA and FRED data refresh nightly via GitHub Actions.</p>
<div class="tech-tags">
  <span class="tech-tag">R</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Shiny</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Golem</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Docker</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">GitHub Actions</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">EIA API</span>
</div>
<div class="project-link"><a href="https://github.com/PassiveAlex/Fin-451-Golem" target="_blank">View on GitHub &rarr;</a></div>
</div>

<div class="project-card">
<div class="project-label">Valuation &middot; NPV@Risk</div>
<h5><a href="projects/Upgrader_Master_Document.html">Upgrader Project Valuation &mdash; Suncor</a></h5>
<p>Full valuation of a $2B Cold Lake Bitumen-to-Synthetic Crude upgrader investment for Suncor using NPV@Risk methodology. Models the SYN-CL upgrading spread with an Ornstein-Uhlenbeck with Jumps (OUJ) stochastic process, simulating 20 years of monthly cash flows across thousands of scenarios. Stress tests include geopolitical shocks, government-backed hydrocracking, and escalating carbon taxation.</p>
<div class="tech-tags">
  <span class="tech-tag">R</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Monte Carlo</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Ornstein-Uhlenbeck</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">DCF</span>
  <span class="tech-tag">Energy Finance</span>
</div>
<div class="project-link"><a href="projects/Upgrader_Master_Document.html">View Report &rarr;</a></div>
</div>

</div>
